title: Silky Ethiopian Chicken Curry
description: A quick, nut‑free, silky chicken curry bursting with Berbere spice and fragrant makrut lime leaves, perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander, 1 tsp mustard, and 1 tsp fennel seeds; toast until fragrant, about 1–2 minutes.
Add the finely chopped onion and cook for 3–4 minutes until translucent.
Stir in minced garlic, ginger, and 1 tbsp tomato paste; cook for 1 minute.
Sprinkle 2 tbsp nut‑free Berbere spice mix over the onions and cook for another minute to bloom the spices.
Add the chicken pieces and brown on all sides, about 4–5 minutes.
Pour in 1 cup chicken broth and 200 ml coconut milk; bring to a gentle simmer, then add torn makrut lime leaves.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in 1 tbsp lemon juice, season with salt to taste, and remove from heat. Discard the lime leaves.
Serve hot over cooked rice or injera, and garnish with chopped cilantro.
